entries.definition: Net revenue = SUM(sales_transactions.total_amount) minus SUM(returns.refund_amount) for returns with status = 'refunded' in the same period (filter returns by return_date). Use exactly this shape: WITH sales AS (SELECT ROUND(SUM(total_amount)::numeric, 2) AS total, COUNT(*) AS transactions_analyzed FROM sales_transactions WHERE <period>), refunds AS (SELECT COALESCE(ROUND(SUM(refund_amount)::numeric, 2), 0) AS total FROM returns WHERE status = 'refunded' AND <period on return_date>) SELECT sales.total - refunds.total AS net_revenue, sales.transactions_analyzed FROM sales, refunds. Put COUNT(*) inside the sales CTE only — never add a bare COUNT(*) to the final SELECT (it mixes an aggregate with non-grouped CTE columns and fails).
Gross revenue = SUM(sales_transactions.total_amount), before subtracting refunds. Plain 'revenue' means gross revenue unless the question says net.

Refunded amount counts only returns with status = 'refunded' (statuses pending, received, approved, rejected are not paid out). Refunded amount = SUM(returns.refund_amount) WHERE status = 'refunded', filtered by returns.return_date.

Return rate = (count of returns excluding status 'rejected') * 100.0 / (count of sales_transactions in the same scope). Scope returns by joining returns.transaction_id = sales_transactions.id when filtering by category or region (returns has no region/category columns). Use NULLIF on the denominator.

Active customer = a customer with at least one purchase in the most recent quarter of available data (Q4 2024): COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM sales_transactions WHERE transaction_date >= '2024-10-01'. Do not count all rows in the customers table.

Churned customer = a customer who purchased in H1 2024 (before '2024-07-01') but has no purchase on or after '2024-07-01'. Use DISTINCT customer_id sets from sales_transactions (e.g., NOT IN / NOT EXISTS on the second-half purchases).
Repeat customer = a customer with 2 or more transactions in the period: GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 on sales_transactions.
Average order value (AOV) = SUM(total_amount) / COUNT(*) over sales_transactions in the period (equivalently AVG(total_amount); one row = one order).

Case resolution time = average of (resolved_at - created_at) over support_cases WHERE resolved_at IS NOT NULL. Report in hours: AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (resolved_at - created_at)) / 3600). Never cast an interval directly to numeric.
Open case backlog counts support_cases with status IN ('open', 'in_progress'). A case is not open once status is 'resolved' or 'closed'.

Region performance is ranked by NET revenue, not gross: per region, SUM(sales_transactions.total_amount) minus SUM(returns.refund_amount) for status = 'refunded' returns attributed to the region via returns.transaction_id = sales_transactions.id.
Units sold = SUM(sales_transactions.quantity), not the count of transaction rows (one transaction can contain multiple units).
The company fiscal year equals the calendar year. FY2024 = 2024-01-01 through 2024-12-31. Fiscal quarters equal calendar quarters.
The returns table has no region or category columns. To scope returns by region, category, or store, join returns.transaction_id = sales_transactions.id and filter on the sales_transactions columns.
Join customers to purchases with customers.customer_id = sales_transactions.customer_id (varchar business key, not the integer id column).
